title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript with TWO selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer AND a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) — Veach–Guibas camera×light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise. Because both estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser.
